data.title: Tasly Pharmaceutical Gets Nod to Trial Drug for Recurrent Glioblastoma
data.description:
China's National Medical Products Administration approved Tasly Pharmaceutical's application to conduct clinical trials on a drug for recurrent glioblastoma, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The P134 cell injection, which was developed by the pharmaceutical company and the Beijing Neurosurgical Institute, will undergo phase 1 trials for patients with the condition.

The pharmaceutical company invested 49.6 million yuan into the research and development of the drug, the filing said.

data.title: Stelar Finds Tin Anomalies at New South Wales Project
data.description:
Stelar Metals discovered tin anomalies at eight prospects at its Trident Project in New South Wales, following a review of historical data, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company said it will recommence lithium exploration at the project once market conditions improve, the filing said.

data.title: OpenLearning Teams Up with Education Center of Australia to Acquire Digital Learning Provider
data.description:
OpenLearning partnered with Education Center of Australia (ECA Group) to acquire the Employability Advantage business, a provider of digital work-readiness content and experiential learning programs, according to an April 11 filing with the Australian bourse.

The Australian Securities Exchange granted the company a waiver to allow the acquisition from ECA Investments Group without shareholder approval, subject to certain disclosures, for a total consideration of AU$1, the filing said.

ECA Group holds 100% of the capital in ECA Investments Group, which is controlled by a substantial shareholder in the company, per the filing.

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

SATURDAY APRIL 12, 2025

Direct nuclear talks to take place between US, Iran - President Donald Trump made a surprise announcement on Monday (April 7) that the United States and Iran were poised to begin direct talks on Tehran's nuclear program, but Iran's foreign minister said the discussions in Oman would be indirect.

Turkey hosts Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Syria's Sharaa and Russia's Lavrov to attend - Turkey hosts the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in the Mediterranean city of Antalya on April 11-13, with various foreign leaders attending including Syria's President Ahmed al-Sharaa and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan is expected to deliver a speech.

Gabon to hold presidential election - Gabon is expected to hold a presidential election in a step towards establishing democratic rule in the Central African country.

Antalya Diplomacy Forum continues

Ecuadoreans go to the polls to select a president in a second round of voting.

MONDAY APRIL 14, 2025

China's President Xi to meet Vietnam's PM in Hanoi as trade conflict with US widens - China's President Xi Jinping is expected to meet Vietnam's Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh in Hanoi as the Chinese leader embarks on a three-nation Southeast Asia tour amid a widening trade conflict with the U.S.

Many government workers face Monday deadline to respond to new Trump buyout offer - Federal workers at a number of government agencies face a Monday deadline to respond to a new buyout offer from the Trump administration.

El Salvador's Bukele meets with Trump - U.S. President Donald Trump welcomes El Salvador President Nayib Bukele to the White House. They are expected to discuss a prison in the Central American nation that has received alleged foreign gang members deported from the United States.

TUESDAY APRIL 15, 2025

Nationwide protests in the US against cuts to Social Security - "Hands Off Social Security!," protests against cuts to Social Security take place across the United States.

China's President Xi Jinping undertakes a state visit to Malaysia

16:00 - Former Peruvian president Humala and wife face sentencing for money laundering - Peruvian former President Ollanta Humala and his wife, Nadine Heredia, accused of money laundering, set to hear sentencing. The charges stem from alleged illegal contributions to their 2006 and 2011 election campaigns, including those from Brazilian company Odebrecht. The Public Prosecutor's Office has requested a 20-year prison sentence for the former president and 26 years and six months for his wife.

WEDNESDAY APRIL 16, 2025

China's President Xi Jinping undertakes a state visit to Malaysia

02:00 - Canadian party leaders debate for election - The leaders of Canada's main political parties gather for a televised French language debate in Montreal

THURSDAY APRIL 17, 2025

Italy's Meloni meets with Trump - Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni meets with U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House.

China's President Xi expected to meet Cambodian PM in Phnom Penh - China's President Xi Jinping pays a state visit to Cambodia where he is expected to meet Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet, according to the Cambodian Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Xi's three-nation Southeast Asia tour comes as he looks to consolidate ties with some of China's closest neighbours as trade tensions with the United States escalate.

01:00 - Canadian party leaders debate for election - The leaders of Canada's main political parties gather for a televised English language debate in Montreal

FRIDAY APRIL 18, 2025

Orthodox Christians mark Good Friday with Via Dolorosa procession in Jerusalem.

Nationwide anti-Trump protests - Thousands of demonstrators in Washington D.C. and around the United States are expected to protest U.S. President Donald Trump and his policies.

SUNDAY APRIL 20, 2025

EASTER SUNDAY - NO EVENTS SCHEDULED

data.title: China's March exports jump on pre-tariff rush, but trade war dims outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • China's exports beat expectations in March
  • Exports up 12.4%, while imports fell 4.3%
  • Trade tensions with U.S. escalate
  • China's trade surplus at $102.64 billion

China's export growth beat expectations in March after factories rushed out shipments before the latest U.S. tariffs took effect, as an escalating Sino-U.S. trade war threatens to sharply restrain growth in the world's second-largest economy.

Exports rose 12.4% year-on-year, handily beating 4.4% growth expected in a Reuters poll of economists. Exports grew 2.3% in January-February.

Inbound shipments fell 4.3%, compared with a 2.0% decrease forecast in a Reuters poll, and an unexpectedly steep contraction of 8.4% at the start of the year.

Trade uncertainties have rocked financial markets this month after U.S. President Donald Trump announced sweeping tariffs on many countries on April 2. Trump unexpectedly paused the higher duties on a dozen economies days later, but slapped even higher levies on China that Beijing has dismissed as "a joke".

Washington has jacked up tariffs on China by an eye-watering 145%, prompting Beijing to lift levies on U.S. goods by 125% in an intensifying trade war between the world's two biggest economies.

Exports have been a lone bright spot in China's economy, which has struggled to mount a solid post-COVID recovery as confidence has remained low in the face of a protracted property crisis and deepening deflationary pressures.

Beijing has vowed to fight to the end against U.S. tariffs and protect the economy from "external shocks", with markets widely expecting authorities to roll out further fiscal and monetary stimulus measures in coming months to underpin growth.

The World Trade Organization has warned the high-stakes Sino-U.S. trade row could cut the shipment of goods between two economies by as much as 80% and severely hurt global growth.

Goldman Sachs last week lowered its forecasts for China's 2025 GDP growth to 4% from 4.5%, citing the effects of tariffs. Citi cut its forecast to 4.2% from 4.7% two days earlier. Their revised forecasts are well below the government's growth target of "around 5%"

China was not alone in stepping up efforts to out-run Trump's tariffs ahead of April.

German exports also rose more than expected in February, signalling world-wide fontloading, with shipments to the U.S. up 8.5% on the month, as did South Korean shipments.

South Korea's exports to China, a leading indicator of the latter's imports, fell 4.1% in March, Korean data showed this month.

China's March trade surplus was $102.64 billion, down slightly from $104.8 billion in December, the most recent comparable reading, but roughly in line with the level recorded a year earlier. This will likely keep the production powerhouse in Trump's sights given that bringing the trade gap down is at the top of his agenda.

data.title: China's March copper imports fall as higher US prices attract more shipments
data.description:
China's imports of unwrought copper and copper products in March declined 1.4% from a year earlier to 467,000 metric tons, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Monday.

For the first three months in 2025, copper imports were down 5.2% at 1.37 million tons, the data showed.

The year-on-year decline in March was attributed to higher copper prices in the U.S., after President Donald Trump began an investigation into copper imports. The higher prices have led to more refined copper shipments to the U.S. instead of China.

The data includes anode, refined, alloy and semi-finished copper products.

Deliverable copper stocks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (CU-STX-SGH) stood at 235,296 tons on March 28, a 12% decrease from the 268,337 tons recorded on February 28.

Imports of copper concentrate stood at 2.39 million tons for March, up 2.6% from the corresponding period last year, customs data showed.

data.title: TSMC first-quarter profit likely soared but Trump policies cloud outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • Analysts expect 54% quarterly profit rise to $10.7 billion
  • Trump has praised TSMC, also threatened it with taxes
  • Earnings call at 0600 GMT on Thursday

TSMC , the main global producer of advanced chips used in artificial intelligence applications, is set to report a 54% leap in first-quarter profit on Thursday, though is also likely to flag risk from trade policies of U.S. President Donald Trump.

The world's largest contract chipmaker, whose customers include Apple and Nvidia , has benefited from a trend towards implementing AI features in online products. But the firm faces headwind not just from Trump's import tariffs but also his criticism of Taiwan's dominance of the chip industry.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co is set to report net profit of T$347.8 billion ($10.74 billion) for the three months through March 31, according to a LSEG SmartEstimate drawn from 17 analysts. SmartEstimates give greater weighting to forecasts from analysts who are more consistently accurate.

That estimate compares to the 2024 first-quarter net profit of T$225.5 billion.

TSMC has been spending billions of dollars on new factories overseas, though it said most manufacturing will remain in Taiwan. It announced a $100 billion investment with Trump at the White House last month, on top of $65 billion pledged for three plants in the U.S. state of Arizona.

Trump has both praised Taiwan's chip industry and threatened it with tariffs.

Last week, he said he had told TSMC it would have to pay a tax of up to 100% if it did not build factories in the U.S. On Sunday, he said the exclusion of smartphones and computers from tariffs on China will be short-lived, pledging a national security trade investigation into the semiconductor sector.

"The company will likely double down on overseas fab investments to mitigate the geopolitical risk, despite two to three percentage points of gross margin dilution for the next five years," said SemiAnalysis analyst Sravan Kundojjala.

"This will likely ensure TSMC gets favourable treatment from the U.S. government and minimise the tariff burden."

Apple's iPhones, given they are mainly made in China, is another risk area for TSMC, said Cathay Futures analyst Venson Tsai.

"If the iPhone can't be sold, then TSMC's chips can't either," Tsai said.

TSMC last week reported a surge in first-quarter revenue in Taiwan dollars, slightly ahead of market expectations. The company gives its revenue outlook in U.S. dollars on its quarterly earnings call, scheduled for 0600 GMT on Thursday.

It will also update its outlook for the current quarter as well as for the full year, including planned capital expenditure for production increases.

On its last earnings call in January, TSMC said it expected capital spending this year to be $38 billion to $42 billion, an increase of as much as 41% from last year.

($1 = 32.3720 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: China's Q1 coal imports fall 0.9% on the year
data.description:
China's January-March coal imports were 114.85 million metric tons, down 0.9% from 115.89 million tons a year earlier, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Monday.

Imports for March were 38.73 million metric tons.

data.title: Australia's Monadelphous rises; Bell Potter raises PT
data.description:
** Shares of engineering company Monadelphous Group rise 0.9% to A$14.95

** Bell Potter raises price target to A$16.2 from A$15.9, retains 'hold' rating; new PT a 9.4% upside to stock's last close

** Brokerage raises FY26 and FY27 revenue estimates to A$2.31 billion ($1.45 billion) and A$2.38 billion to reflect increased Zenviron project delivery

** Expects Zenviron joint venture to increasingly contribute to NPAT over FY25-27 through heightened near-term project tendering

** Analysts rate the stock 'hold' on average; their median price target is A$16.1 - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock up 6.8% YTD, including moves in current session

($1 = 1.5898 Australian dollars)

data.title: Indonesia Forex Reserves Hit Record High
data.description:
Indonesia’s foreign exchange reserves rose to USD 157.1 billion in March 2025, up from USD 154.5 billion in February.

This marks the highest level on record, bolstered by tax and service revenue inflows and foreign loan withdrawals by the government.

The increase occurred despite Bank Indonesia’s ongoing efforts to stabilize the rupiah, amid persistent global financial market uncertainty.

The central bank’s intervention measures underscore the balancing act between currency stability and reserve accumulation in the current volatile global environment.

data.title: Euro Manganese's New Chairman Joins Early as Fundraising Timeline Gets Updated; Shares Fall 7%
data.description:
Euro Manganese appointed Rick Anthon as chairman of the board of directors, effective April 11, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Anthon's appointment was brought forward due to the updated timeline for the company's fundraising.

Euro Manganese's previously announced CA$11.2 million, or AU$12.3 million, financing is now conditionally approved by the TSX Venture Exchange. A final approval is subject to the company meeting other requirements, including shareholders' approval.

In the same filing, the mining company confirmed the revised launch date of its AU$1.5 million share purchase plan on April 28 for shareholders on the record date of March 5, the filing stated.

Company shares fell 7% in recent trade.

data.title: China March rare earth exports at 5,666.3 tonnes, customs data shows
data.description:
China's exports in March of rare earths jumped 20.31% from a year earlier to 5,666.3 metric tons, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Monday.

In the first quarter of 2025, exports of the group of 17 minerals from the world's largest producer rose 5.1% from a year earlier to 14,177.6 tons, the customs data showed.

data.title: Visionflex Group Signs Agreement to Supply Technology for Virtual Nursing Project
data.description:
Visionflex Group signed a binding agreement with Amplar Home Health to supply its virtual care technology solution for a project that will explore the integration of virtual nursing services into aged care homes to provide personalized clinical care and support to on-site staff, according to a Monday bourse filing.

Amplar Home Health is the health service delivery arm of Medibank

The project will run from March until June 2027, and it will include up to 30 residential aged-care homes across Australia, with phased deployment over 15 months.

The total contract value amounts to around AU$1 million, subject to site rollout. The contract includes a one-off hardware revenue of around AU$750,000, as well as a software subscription revenue of around AU$250,000 over the trial period.

Medibank's shares gained about 1% in recent Monday trade.

data.title: Saudi AZM Lands Sustainability Website, Application Operations Project in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Saudi AZM for Communication and Information Technology (SASE:9534) secured a project award from the Royal Commission for Riyadh City in Saudi Arabia.

The project covers the operations of sustainability Riyadh websites and applications, the Saudi Arabian information technology company said Sunday.

The project is valued at over 5% of the company's total revenue for the full-year 2024.

data.title: Macquarie lowers PT for Air New Zealand on weaker capacity recovery, global uncertainty
data.description:
** Macquarie cuts price target for Air New Zealand to NZ$0.83 from NZ$0.85

** Brokerage forecasts earnings to lower in FY26 and FY27 by 17% and 5% respectively on weaker capacity and demand recovery

** Says volatile global conditions stemming from trade war uncertainties to worsen consumer confidence which could spillover to co's markets

** Stock is up 0.9%, in line with positive sentiment seen in local markets, with New Zealand's S&P/NZX 50

** YTD, shares down 2.50%

data.title: Australia 10-Year Bond Yield Falls
data.description:
Australia’s 10-year government bond yield dipped to around 4.41% as investors look ahead to the RBA’s meeting minutes and labor market data this week for further clues on monetary policy.

Although the central bank held rates steady this month, it softened its stance on future cuts, citing easing core inflation.

Mounting tariff concerns and rising US-China tensions—posing risks to Australia’s export-driven economy, particularly with China as its largest trading partner—have further fueled expectations of a more accommodative approach.

Still, Governor Michele Bullock recently cautioned that it’s “too early” to gauge the trade war’s impact on rates and downplayed the prospect of a double cut in May.

Nevertheless, markets remain fully priced for a 25bps reduction next month, with some even leaning toward a half-point move.

data.title: Hengrui Medicine Gets Nod to Trial Anti-Tumor Drug; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengrui Medicine , through its subsidiaries, received approval from China's National Medical Products Administration to conduct clinical trials on an anti-tumor drug, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Sunday.

The SHR-3792 injection will be tested on advanced solid tumors.

The pharmaceutical company invested 32.7 million yuan into the research and development of the drug, the filing said.

The company's shares jumped 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Sichuan Chuantou Energy 2024 Profit Up 2.5%, Revenue Rises 8.5%
data.description:
Sichuan Chuantou Energy posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 4.51 billion yuan, up 2.5% from 4.40 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share ticked down to 0.9294 yuan from 0.9317 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The energy company's revenue increased 8.5% year over year to 1.61 billion yuan from 1.48 billion yuan.

Sichuan Chuantou Energy declared a dividend of 0.40 yuan per share.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: China Trade Surplus Above Estimates
data.description:
China's trade surplus surged to USD 102.64 billion in March 2025, up from USD 58.65 billion in the same period a year earlier, exceeding market expectations of USD 77 billion.

The sharp increase was largely driven by a 12.4% year-on-year surge in exports—the fastest rise in four months—far surpassing market forecasts of a 4.4% gain, as factories rushed to ship goods ahead of U.S. President Trump's upcoming tariffs.

Meanwhile, imports fell by 4.3%, compared to market expectations of a 2% drop and following an 8.4% slump in the January–February period.

NOTES: The State Bank of Vietnam sets the mid-point rate on a daily basis and allows dollar/dong transactions to move in a band of +/- 5% around the mid point.

The dong's exchange rate against other currencies is not restricted by a band. Interbank offered rates are the latest indicative bid/ask prices, quoted from market sources.

One tael is equivalent to 37.5 grams or 1.21 troy ounces. SJC gold prices are quoted by state-owned Saigon Jewelry Co.

For more interbank rate fixings released at 0400 GMT, click on (VNIBOR-1).

data.title: China's March copper imports fall 1.4% y/y to 467,000 tons
data.description:
China's imports of unwrought copper and copper products in March declined 1.4% year-on-year to 467,000 metric tons, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Monday.

For the first three months in 2025, copper imports were down 5.2% at 1.37 million tons, the data showed.

data.title: Malaysian palm tracks rival Chicago soyoil lower
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures fell on Monday, tracking weakness in rival soyoil at the Chicago market.

The benchmark June palm oil contract on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange lost 31 ringgit, or 0.74%, to 4,181 ringgit ($944.65) a metric ton in early trade.

The contract fell 2.63% last week.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) added 0.65%, while its palm oil contract lost 0.07%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) fell 0.68%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* India's palm oil imports in March rose about 14% from the previous month to 424,599 tons, a trade body said on Friday.

* Independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia said exports of Malaysian palm oil products for April 1-10 rose 52.8% to 301,113 tons, while according to cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services it rose 29.3% to 323,160 tons.

* Oil prices fell on Monday on concerns the escalating trade war between the United States and China would weaken global economic growth and dent fuel demand.

* Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, slipped 0.14% against the U.S. dollar, making the commodity cheaper for buyers holding foreign currencies.

MARKET NEWS

* Wall Street share futures rallied in Asia after the White House exempted smartphones and computers from "reciprocal" U.S. tariffs, though gains were limited as President Donald Trump warned levies were still likely at some point.

DATA/EVENTS

1000 EU Reserve Assets Total

($1 = 4.4260 ringgit)

data.title: FAA Says Helicopter Tour Company in Fatal Hudson Crash Will Halt Flights
data.description:
By Ginger Adams Otis

New York Helicopter, the tour operator behind the Hudson River crash that killed a Spanish family of five and a pilot, is shutting down, the Federal Aviation Administration said Sunday.

The FAA said the company will stop operations immediately, and the FAA will conduct a review of New York Helicopter's license and safety record. The National Transportation Safety Board's probe into the cause of last week's crash will continue, the FAA said.

"Safety is the FAA's number one priority and we will not hesitate to act to protect the flying public," the agency said on social media.

Mike Roth, owner of the tour company, said he was unaware of the FAA announcement when reached by phone Sunday. "I did not authorize that," he said.

New York Helicopter operated the Bell 206 that crashed into the Hudson River on April 10, killing all six people on board.

The aircraft was giving a tour ride to Agustín Escobar Cañadas, 49 years old, and Mercè Camprubí Montal, 39, who both worked for Siemens, and their three young children. The family's visit to New York from Spain was timed to coincide with what would have been their daughter's ninth birthday. The pilot, Sean Johnson, 36, was the sixth victim.

The aircraft plunged into the river on its way back to Lower Manhattan after a short trip up the Hudson to the George Washington Bridge. It landed in the water around Hoboken, New Jersey.

The crash has renewed debate over helicopter trips over New York City, which includes sightseeing tours and commuter flights to local airports and nearby destinations such as the Hamptons.

A deadly 2018 helicopter crash in the East River that killed five people prompted the city to ban sightseeing tours out of the city that use helicopters without doors. That accident also led to new federal regulations related to the safety restraints used for open-door helicopter flights. Thursday's crash involved a helicopter with doors.

Write to Ginger Adams Otis at Ginger.AdamsOtis@wsj.com

data.title: Vietnam tells firms to tighten control on origins of goods and materials
data.description:
Vietnam has told firms to tighten controls on the origins of goods and materials and to tackle fraud, according to a ministry document that was seen by Reuters, as the country tries to avoid being hit with a 46% tariff rate by the Trump administration.

The ministry also told firms to diversify their sources of materials and avoid relying on a single source, according to the ministry document, which was signed by Minister Nguyen Hong Dien on Thursday.

The call for companies to ensure correct origin-of-goods details comes after Washington raised concerns about Chinese goods being sent to America with "Made in Vietnam" labels, and as Hanoi tries to negotiate a U.S. trade deal.

"The international trade situation is evolving in a fast and unpredictable manner, in the context of the U.S. seeking to impose tariffs on several countries, including Vietnam," the ministry document said.

Last week, the Vietnamese government said the United States and Vietnam had agreed to start talks for a trade agreement after the U.S. announced a 90-day pause on its global "reciprocal" tariffs, which include a 46% levy on Vietnam.

Vietnam is a major regional manufacturing base for many Western companies, and is heavily reliant on materials from China. Last year, Vietnam had a trade surplus of more than $123 billion with the U.S., its largest export market.

data.title: Equinox Starts Legal Proceedings for Review of Heritage Committee's Decision on Western Australia Project
data.description:
Equinox Resources started legal proceedings in the Supreme Court of Western Australia to review a decision by the Aboriginal Cultural Heritage Committee (ACHC) about its Hamersley Iron Ore Project in Western Australia, according to an April 11 filing with the Australian bourse.

The review challenges the Minister for Aboriginal Affairs' refusal of the company's application for an infill drilling program at the project, citing newly identified ethnographic sites, the filing said.

The company has submitted detailed documents to the Court, including affidavits and expert evidence, and will argue that the case should be reconsidered, the filing added.

A directions hearing on the matter is expected by the end of July, the company said.
